prompt: Using the provided reference image of the packaged washed mozuku seaweed, transform it into a cute Japanese recipe infographic poster that teaches how to eat it properly. Keep the actual product recognizable by showing the package photo near the top and reuse the mozuku itself in small food/process photos, but redesign everything else as a bright, clean, blue-and-pink editorial layout. Add a cheerful anime catmaid guide character with pink hair and cat ears appearing multiple times as the instructor. Create a bold Japanese headline at the top: 「洗いもずくを 美味しく食べる手順書」 and a worker/name tag reading 「作業者：きらりあ・ネオン」. Build a 6-step vertical instruction flow on the left/middle with numbered circles 1 through 6 and arrows between them. The 6 step titles should be: 1「状態確認」 2「軽く水洗い」 3「水気を切る」 4「食べやすくカット」 5「味付け」 6「盛り付けて完成」. For each step, include a short Japanese explanation and a supporting image/illustration showing the mozuku at that stage; specifically include 1 package/close-up state check image, 1 rinsing-in-colander image, 1 draining colander image, 1 cutting-with-kitchen-scissors image, 3 seasoning option mini-boxes under step 5, and 1 finished bowl image under step 6. Add a right-side panel titled 「おすすめ具材」 with exactly 7 ingredients shown as small labeled illustrations: きゅうり, 長芋, オクラ, みょうが, 大葉, しらす, 豆腐. Add another right-side recommendation panel titled 「ネオンちゃんおすすめ」 containing exactly 2 dish cards labeled A and B: A「もずく酢きゅうり」 and B「もずくスープ」, each with a bowl image and ingredient list in Japanese. Add a bottom-right caution box titled 「注意」 with exactly 3 bullet points: 「洗いすぎない」「水気をしっかり切る」「開封後は早めに食べる」. Add a friendly footer banner in Japanese encouraging enjoyment of mozuku. Style it like a highly readable supermarket handout / cooking guide infographic with rounded boxes, glossy accents, clear hierarchy, and vibrant kawaii appeal.
